title: Roboquest VR (3151760)
text:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Roboquest VR
- Steam AppID of the game: 3151760

## System Information
- GPU: RX 7900 XTX
- Video driver version: Mesas 25.2.7
- Kernel version: 6.17.8-273-tkg-eevdf
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/Patola/e56c58f70d70e30c6accf3e71b352a18
- Proton version: 10.0-3

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

## Symptoms 

Using the official Steam Link via SteamVR 2.13.7, when the game starts, it starts playing some music and the monitor screen goes blank, but SteamVR freezes/crashes and the client says the server is not responding. Then I have to force-quit the game and it often crashes Steam.

This also happens if I play the game via ALVR (another streaming software that couples itself to SteamVR).

It doesn't happen with WiVRn, which provides a separate openxr and openvr (xrizer) backends. The game starts normally.

## Reproduction

Just get in VR with SteamVR and press play on the game.

## Proton log
I am attaching two logs:
1) regular log with `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%`
2) VR-focused log with `PROTON_LOG=+vrclient,+openxr,+file %command%`

[steam-3151760-vrclient-openxr-file.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23696226/steam-3151760-vrclient-openxr-file.zip)
[steam-3151760.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23696227/steam-3151760.log)

title: Far Cry 4 (298110)
text:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Far Cry 4
- Steam AppID of the game: 298110

## System Information
- GPU: PowerColor Radeon RX Vega 64 Red Devil 8GB
- Driver/LLVM version: Mesa 19.0.0-devel (git-e2ae5f2f0a), LLVM 8.0.0
- Kernel version: 4.20.0-rc7-mainline
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/Holzhaus/837a0fb4e7ee0783effc43968a31e73f
- Proton version: 3.16.6

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.



## Symptoms 

A Uplay window with the title "Looking for updates" appears, accompanied by an error message box that tells us that Uplay has detected an unrecoverable error and must shut down.

![Screenshot](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1834516/50453423-bf16a500-0940-11e9-9152-eed012cc6792.png)

Log file: [steam-298110.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/2710662/steam-298110.log)

Uplay crash dump (GZipped): 
[Uplay.exe_4.8.1.3617_2018-12-26_18-53-02.dmp.gz](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/2710674/Uplay.exe_4.8.1.3617_2018-12-26_18-53-02.dmp.gz)



## Reproduction

Install Far Cry 4 and click the "Play" button.

title: Apex Legends: No incoming in-game voice chat
text:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Apex Legends
- Steam AppID of the game: 1172470

## System Information
- GPU: Radeon 6900XT
- Driver/LLVM version: OpenGL version string: 4.6 (Compatibility Profile) Mesa 22.1.4
- Kernel version: 5.18.15-200.fc36.x86_64
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): [System information](https://gist.github.com/morgaesis/e549f32a01d8b68886c1bfe88c55fe98)
- Proton version: Proton Experimental

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.


[steam-1172470.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/9277419/steam-1172470.log)


## Symptoms 


## Reproduction

1. Install Steam (system) on Fedora (silverblue?) and Apex Legends
2. Enter match
3. Use voice comms/voice chat
4. Team mates can hear me, but no audio from team mates

I can hear team mates using in-game VC (voice chat) on Windows, team mates seem to hear me, Discord VC works fine. Discord team mate has to relay whatever in-game VC is going on. I can see the speaker icon showing that team mate is speaking in-game.

metadata.issue_title: Apex Legends: No incoming in-game voice chat

title: Warhammer 40k Darktide crashes during play, closes steam and causes gdm to restart
text:
# Compatibility Report
- Warhammer 40k Darktide
- db# 1361210

## System Information
- Radeon RX 7900 xtx
- Mesa version 24.0.3
- Kernel version: 6.8.0
- System info...https://gist.github.com/DarkeOne/802f0de7400e43271614e71ba6fb59f6


- Proton version:8.0.5 to 9.0, even GE 9.7

## I confirm:
- [ X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [ X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

[steam-1361210.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/15811975/steam-1361210.log)




## Symptoms
This game runs great on my system-right up until it crashes. The game freezes, screen goes black, and I'm dumped to a login screen because I _think_ GDM crashed too (newb here). Steam also crashes as well. If I try to run the game again, it simply won't run, generating a file with an error "_object 'libgamemodeauto.so.0' from LD_PRELOAD cannot be preloaded (cannot open shared object file): ignored."_

It doesn't matter what Proton version I use, what graphic setting I run (low or high), it happens. Sometimes right at the beginning of a match, sometimes after two or three matches. It does this on any linux distro I run (Pop OS, Fedora KDE, Nobara)

I really like this game, and I hate Windows. Any help is appreciated. Thanks for reading!


Steam Runtime Diagnostics...

https://gist.github.com/DarkeOne/ad54eef4780002ad1929ffb43afa3302

metadata.issue_title: Warhammer 40k Darktide crashes during play, closes steam and causes gdm to restart
